entry.title: Loud And Opaque
entry.summary: You know what I love? Writing in generalities when people need…
entry.content:
You know what I love?

Writing in generalities when people need specifics.

You might hear a lot of about adding value.

Just add value.

I sure do.

And you might be wondering, like I do, what that all means.

Well, I'll tell you.

It's just a way to sound smart.

To sound influency.

To sound like you're giving advice when all you're really doing is just being vague.

Add value is like a newer Always Be Closing.

ABC!

But forget all that.

You want to add value?

Help.

Help people.

Help animals.

Help your surroundings.

Help the universe.

Help causes you still believe in.

Here's hoping that includes yourself.

Just my value add for the day.

Invoice is in your inbox.

K thx.

entry.title: Make That Change
entry.summary: 🎵 Who's bad? 🎶 We live in this weird world, man.
entry.content:
🎵 Who's bad? 🎶

We live in this weird world, man.

A strange universe.

To quote Billy Shat:

"And I love it."

But here's the thing.

A lot of the "heroes" my history classes taught me about are now taught to today's kids as villains.

A lot of icons becomes crooks.

Nothing changes but our perspective.

Just with the passage of time.

But, like all things, there are exceptions to the rule.

That prove the rule.

Sometimes, a villain can become a hero again.

Take Michael Jackson.

If there ever is a person I'm glad is having a comeback (of sorts) it's Michael.

The first MJ.

The best MJ.

I'm a bit ashamed to say when I was younger, MJ was a butt of a lot of jokes.

But he's had this odd redemption story.

Although that's not entirely fair, because it implies he did something wrong in the first place.

Which now, it seems, he never did.

That he was always good.

Now, the suspicious side of me is thinking, hmm, all this good stuff about MJ is coming out just coincidentally alongside the release of his movie.

But let's ignore that.

Put that thought in a corner and point my finger at it.

Stay.

...

But weird timing, right?

...

And focus on the positive.

It's ok to like Mike again.

You ever hear his real voice?

Nevermind.

I am just happy that we don't just stomp on graves anymore.

Some graves we can repair.

We can apologize to.

We can visit. 

We can remember.

Aww, MJ, the world did you dirty, man. 

I'm thinking this is the world's collective "our bad" moment.

And it's good to know, even in these absurd times, that we can admit we were wrong. 

A good, bad, man.

Hero.

Never the "e" word many wanted him to be.

🎵 You know I'm bad. 🎶

📻

Sorry for ever doubting.

To BadMFs.

Keeping the good good.

entry.title: We're Both Learning Together
entry.summary: Good people. Yes, I'm talking to you. You good? It occurs…
entry.content:
Good people.

Yes, I'm talking to you.

You good?

It occurs to me, as such things do, that good people are too quiet.

Too hesitant.

To put their foots forward.

To raise their hands.

To say, "Hey, look at me."

It makes sense.

Humility.

Privacy.

Meek.

As a result of their silence, there's this sea of foot-forwarded, hand-raised, look-at-me jackarses.

Just an ocean of 'em.

An ocean!

An ocean of jackarses.

🌊

So much so that you start believing maybe that's all there is out there.

But I'm here to tell you something.

There's a lot more good out there than not.

I believe that.

Do you?

As a believer, as a father, I must.

The good far outnumber the others.

The good far outnumber the others.

The good far outnumber the others.

It just doesn't seem that way anytime you turn on your technology.

But I hope you see it every time you turn it off.

I went to a store opening the other day.

A fancy little fusion joint.

And it was a soft opening.

A bunch of young folk running around and messing up.

A dress rehearsal in front of a live studio audience.

So eager, optimistic, to get it right.

Nervous, hopeful, and trying.

Giving a darn.

Got an old(ish) man rooting for them.

And for a lot of you.

More than I sometimes think.

To the quiet good.

Get louder.

📣

Thank you for giving a sheet.

entry.title: Why For
entry.summary: I'm a sucker for a ballad-ized version of a hard song.
entry.content:
I'm a sucker for a ballad-ized version of a hard song.

I sometimes do it myself.

I'll take a song like "Let the Bodies Hit the Floor" and turn it into a country song.

Like Tracy Lawrence sang it.

Floooooooooooor.

🤠🎸

entry.title: It's Never Personal, Always
entry.summary: You ever watch one of them true crime shows? C'mon, I…
entry.content:
You ever watch one of them true crime shows?

C'mon, I know you have.

They sometimes start it with a recording of an intense, frantic, 911 call.

Person on the line having the worst day of their life.

Here's the part that gets me though.

When the dispatcher is rude.

Or offended.

A plain ol' jerk.

Victim freaking out.

Operator sighing and annoyed.

Even pissed.

Taking sheet personal.

Basically, "I get you're going through something right now, but can you be nicer? Answer my questions in a less emotional way. How hard is that?"

😠

Dispatchers have an unenviable task every time they clock in.

I get that.

But I think, what a select few may need to work on, is remembering:

It's never about them.

If there's ever a job where it ain't about you, it's as a friggin' 911 operator.

Maybe we just recruit ex-NYC cab drivers and have them be emergency dispatchers nationwide.

They've heard it all.

And they can take it.

They wouldn't even have to move.

Just do that sheet from home.

Distributed workforce!

Shout out to the vast majority of dispatchers who do their job well and with empathy.

☎️

And to old cabbies.

🚕

Thank you for your service.

entry.title: We Still Do
entry.summary: You ever watch videos about some old retired Marine boxer knocking…
entry.content:
You ever watch videos about some old retired Marine boxer knocking out some would-be crook?

Like this old fella you wouldn't think much of looking at, just rocking a fool to sleep.

Nighty night.

I would watch them in silent admiration.

Good for you, old man.

You still got it.

Hands are the last thing to go.

And I suppose that's the fate of every man blessed to age past their youth.

When they reach a seasoning where other men no longer find them dangerous upon sight.

And I know that there's a whole school of thought about how being dismissed as weak is actually a benefit, but I dunno.

I dunno.

Thankfully, we have all these force multipliers.

Makes a fight even, even when it ain't.

I'd encourage all of you to look into them, if you haven't.

Follow the law, of course.

...

I do wonder, if I'm honest, if I still got it.

I wonder if you do, too.

...

May you and I never have the need to find out.

But if we do, may we find we still do.

And then some.

🤜🤛

"I need you mountain lion mean."

entry.title: Who You Trying To Get Crazy With, Ese?
entry.summary: Remember the video game, The Sims? Turns out, we're living in…
entry.content:
Remember the video game, The Sims?

Turns out, we're living in it.

That's what science wants you to believe.

At least, it's the most likely of scenarios, they say.

The most likely scenario is we are a simulation inside a simulation inside a simulation.

Like how you can play a video game in a video game.

Sims all the way down.

This might make you upset.

But me?

I'm dancin'.

Why?

Because I'm the baddest gamer out here.

At least I was.

When I was a kid in NYC, other kids and adults would watch me play.

Back in the day of standup systems.

At the corner bodegas.

Diners.

Liquor stores.

A lot of them would feed me quarters so I could keep playing.

And a few would stack quarters on the console (normally done to signify you had next), for these fine folks they wanted me to play for hours so they bankrolled me.

Venture backed before I hit double digits.

Where was I?

Sim City.

Oh right.

So life's a vidja game.

And I'm cool with that.

Also, our reality is not locally real.

So there's that, too.

Which is loco.

And, distance is fake.

Or at least, an illusion.

You and something on the edge of the universe can be, most likely are, part of the same dang system.

Which makes no sense.

So let's just take a minute to pause here.

Let go of the controller.

Drop it, I said.

Now, step back.

Take a deep breath.

If this is all a game, how you doing?

Is your PC doing well?

How is Player One?

And if you looked at the world around you as video game characters, maybe the world makes a little more sense.

There's a bit of GTA, Red Dead, Madden, Harvest Moon, and No Man's Sky all out there.

To name a few.

So what's your game?

And how you doing in it?

And, if this is all one, who's the dev?

What studio is behind all this?

🕹

Wow.

entry.title: Fill In The
entry.summary: After taking a few days off, I thought I might have…
entry.content:
After taking a few days off, I thought I might have something built up to write about.

Be raring to go.

But here I am, staring at this dang blinking cursor.

As a dad.

As a husband.

I seek certain things for my family.

A good life.

Not an easy life.

A good life.

A good, long, healthy, asymmetric, blessed life.

And that's the part that's hard.

Maybe I default to easy too much.

Because you have to, to some degree, allow the not-so-good times to come.

It's part of equation.

And so I've become a doorman of sorts.

Letting some things in that I would normally not, just because it is part of the learning and growing from experience.

Growing pains.

True wisdom is knowing what to let in, and what to keep out.

So what are the not good things we let in?

...

...

...

I think everyone has the right to decide how they lead their families.

So I'll leave my ways out.

But I will say this:

Everything good has a component of the opposite, err, opposing it.

And what needs to stay out?

This, too, you have to decide.

...

...

...

Think permanent. Uneraseable. Unforgivable. You know, the big uns.

[Looks at you, with furrowed brow:]

Got ID?

👤

entry.title: Hearing Aid
entry.summary: Sundays are harder days to write. I don't know why. Maybe…
entry.content:
Sundays are harder days to write.

I don't know why.

Maybe it's because I should take the day off.

A day of rest.

But this is not really laborious.

This is play time.

And so I sits.

So I writes.

Oh, had a miracle happen the other day.

Whole family in our vehicle.

Driving around after some soaking of sun.

After some discussion, we decided to hit the local grocery store last minute so we turned down a side road.

It's that season so lots of construction.

We are waiting at a red at a T. 

Can only go left or right.

We're in the left turn only lane.

Light turns green, I look left and right, all clear, and I start to go.

Now the lane I'm turning onto has two lanes. I usually turn left into the lane on the right, since I'll have to make a right soon after into the store.

Make sense?

So I'm turning and I hear my wife say, calmly but authoritatively, "Turn left. Turn left."

So I turn it tight to the left, getting into the left side of the left lane, and we see a pickup truck fly by us just barely missing our front bumper and right side. 

Lady ran a red.

She never stopped.

Didn't seem to even slow down.

My wife says she gave us a nasty look.

Typical, right?

She had a big dog sticking its head out by her side, so we hypothesize maybe she was distracted. 

But who knows.

Here's the weird part.

Both my son and my wife agree that my wife never said a word.

She remembers gasping and raising her hand up to the right side of her window in an attempt to shield herself.

But didn't say a word.

And my son agrees saying she said nothing.

But what I remember vividly and distinctly is she said, "Turn left. Turn left."

Twice.

And not panicked.

Calm.

Direct.

Matter of factly.

Just do it.

So I did.

Hard.

And the flying dog lady truck missed us.

We had a terrific boost of adrenaline and spent the rest of our day just praising and in a bit of shock.

Thankful.

Near misses are too often and quickly forgotten.

Or at least, not fully appreciated.

I don't want to do that.

So I'll write it down here. 

To remind me to cherish it.

What's weird is this isn't the first time for me.

I had another similar miracle happen to me decades ago.

When I was a teenager.

Also on the road.

A voice told me to stop.

So I did.

But that time, I was in the car all by myself.

entry.title: Anyone Feel A Draft?
entry.summary: I started writing about two things and erased them both. One…
entry.content:
I started writing about two things and erased them both.

One was about being vigilant outside as we approach warmer seasons.

The other was about Day 3 of the NFL Draft.

I erased them both.

I don't know why.

I will say, be careful, out there.

And I love Day 3 of the NFL Draft.

I feel like we should have corporate drafts for the Fortune 500.

Scouts in college classrooms.

An academic combine in auditoriums.

Boardrooms watching highlights of volunteer tape.

Talking heads arguing over who Nvidia will take in the first round.

Actually, that sounds terrible.

Or does it already happen?
